steps.title: Spot the secure legacy
steps.content: The phrase “will always be remembered” establishes that Chojnowska-Liskiewicz's place in history is permanent—a baseline for comparison.

steps.title: Choose the opposite quality
steps.content: C) uncertain expresses that Nancy's legacy is more ambiguous and less firmly established than someone with a singular, memorable achievement. A, B, and D all fail: deserving addresses unrelated worthiness, conspicuous suggests prominence contradicting the security comparison, and enduring wrongly implies her legacy lasts longer. This matches choice C — C is correct.
